message:
perf(pager): short-circuit empty clears on publication + flat-slot tables

Both `AtomicPublishedPages::clear` and `FlatPageSlots::clear` walked every
slot on every call (65,535 and up to power-of-two capacity respectively),
doing an AcqRel atomic swap on each even when the table was already empty
or the slot was already free. Under MT-writer contention the pager
publication plane is cleared on each rollback/retry cycle; 2026-04-23 post-T1
profile at 8 threads showed:

- `PublishedPages::clear`        14.61% self-time
- `PublishedPagerState::new`      1.13%
- `Arc<PublishedPagerState>::drop_slow`  0.98%
- `publish_truncate_checkpoint`   0.80%
- `FlatPageSlots::clear`          3.02%
- Total publication machinery:  ~20% self-time

Changes:
- `AtomicPublishedPages::clear`: early-return when `page_count == 0`; add a
  Relaxed pre-filter before the expensive AcqRel swap so already-empty
  slots are a single load instead of an RMW cycle.
- `FlatPageSlots::clear`: same pattern — early-return when `count == 0`
  (still resetting the eviction cursor); Relaxed pre-filter before the
  AcqRel swap on each slot.

Validation (mt-mvcc-bench --threads=8 --rows-per-thread=500 --iters=10,
2026-04-23 18:51 UTC on ts2):

| metric | pre | post |
|---|---:|---:|
| `PublishedPages::clear` self-time | 14.61% | absent from top 10 |
| `FlatPageSlots::clear` self-time  |  3.02% | absent from top 10 |
| total Published* self-time        | 17.5%  | 3.53% (~80% reduction) |
| typical 8t fs_wps                 |  ~5k   | ~5k (ceiling unchanged) |

The throughput ceiling did not move, because the contention that drives
frequent `clear()` calls is still present — these patches reclaim CPU
wasted in the clear loop itself. The underlying coordination bottleneck
(the MVCC page-lock or publication queue serializing writers) is a
separate structural target and is not addressed here. Relevant hand-off:
`tests/artifacts/perf/profiling-handoff-20260423T155542Z/` (T3 family).

Evidence: /data/tmp/fsqlite-mt-post-t1t2t7-184420 (pre),
/data/tmp/fsqlite-t3b-validation-185110 (post).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
